name: Refrigerator Coleslaw
directions: Toss the cabbage, onion and carrots together in a large bowl.
Boil the remaining ingredients together and pour over the vegetables.
Toss just once and let sit at room temperature 1 and 1/2 hours.
Toss salad once more, cover, and store in the refrigerator overnight before serving.

name: Ramen Broccoli Slaw
directions: Mix slaw, chopped onions, noodles only from Ramen noodles (crush them with your hands), almonds and sunflower seeds together in bowl.
Make dressing w/oil, vinegar, sugar, and add the 2 packets of flavoring from the Ramen noodles.
Add dressing to your taste- I used about half.
Note: Instead of white vinegar and sugar, I used 1/2 cup of flavored rice vinegar.

similar.name: Chinese Chicken Flavored Ramen Noodles Cole Slaw

name: Stir-fried Chinese Cabbage
directions: Stir together rice wine, soy sauce, sesame oil, and ginger root in a small bowl; set aside.
Heat the oil in a wok over medium-high heat.
Add red pepper flakes and minced garlic, and stir-fry until it becomes aromatic but not scorched.
Add shredded cabbage and stir-fry until limp, but not mushy.
Add contents of seasoning bowl and cook, stirring, 1-2 minutes more, until liquid is almost absorbed.
Sprinkle with toasted sesame seeds and serve.

name: Red & Green Coleslaw
directions: COMBINE cabbage, carrots, parsley, onion and raisins, set aside.
COMBINE coleslaw dressing and apple jelly; add to salad and toss well.

name: My Mom's Special Coleslaw
directions: Mix together the cream and Miracle Whip.
Add the vinegar, sugar, salt and pepper, mixing well.
Pour over shredded cabbage, mixing well.
Cover and let sit, in refrigerator, for at least 1 hour.
Mix before serving.
NOTE: Sometimes, just to make the salad stretch for company, she would add shredded carrots, or raisins.

name: Light Creamy Coleslaw
directions: Stir all dressing ingredients together in a large bowl.
Add vegetables and stir to coat well.

name: My Favorite Coleslaw
directions: Combine onion and sugar in bowl.
Let stand for 30 minutes.
Add remaining ingredients, except cabbage, stir well.
Cover and refrigerate.
Before serving, add cabbage.
Toss.

name: Coleslaw - for Low-Carb Diets
directions: NOTE: You may substitute 1/2 cup cream and 2 tbs mayo for the 1/2-3/4 cups mayo.
Thinly slice cabbage with knife.
Cut shreds into 1-inch pieces.
Put in bowl.
Add all other ingredients and stir to mix well.
Chill; stir before serving.

name: Zesty Asian Slaw
directions: In a large bowl, combine all ingredients for salad except noodles.
Stir well to combine.
In another bowl, add all ingredients for dressing, including reserved spice package from noodles, mixing well till sugar is dissolved.
Pour the dressing over the slaw, toss to coat well.
Let marinate for at least 15 minutes.
Add noodles just before serving.

name: Southern Style Coleslaw
directions: Mix cole slaw greens with onion in a large mixing bowl.
Mix sour cream, mayonnaise, sugar, dry mustard, seasoned salt and pepper in a small mixing bowl until smooth.
Add the sauce to the Cole slaw greens and toss thoroughly.
Add paprika (for a nice color) and celery seed (for texture).
Mix again and refrigerate for at least two hours before serving.

name: Oriental Coleslaw
directions: Dice onions, mix with slaw, and set aside.
melt butter in skillet, add seeds and noodles and while constantly stirring, saute until lightly browned.
set aside.
put sugar, vinegar, oil and soy sauce in jar and shake up until well mixed.
just before serving, mix everything together and serve.

name: Coleslaw
directions: Combine shredded cabbage and carrot.
Add remaining ingredients and stir until well combined.

name: Coleslaw
directions: Mix together the vegetables in a large bowl.
Whisk the dressing ingredients together, pour over the vegetables and mix well.
Chill for about an hour before serving.

name: Amy's Coleslaw
directions: Shred 3/4 of cabbage.
Slice remaining cabbage into ribbons and chunkier pieces (you don't want it all finely shredded).
Peel and shred carrot.
In a large bowl, combine all ingredients thoroughly.
Keep in refrigerator; the flavors will mingle and it will taste even better the next day.

name: Cabbage Salad
directions: Toast sesame seeds and almonds in oven at 350 degrees for 5-10 minutes.
Mix, sugar, salad oil, vinegar, salt, pepper and seasoning package to make salad dressing.
Combine all ingredients and toss at the last minute.

name: Sweet & Sour Red Cabbage
directions: Combine all ingredients in a large skillet. Toss together gently.
Add enough water to cover the vegetable mixture.
Bring to a low boil, then reduce heat and cook over medium heat.
Stir occasionally, for 6 to 8 minutes, or just until cabbage is tender.

name: My Secret Ingredient Coleslaw
directions: Chop cabbage,carrots,onion and apple in food processor and transfer to large mixing bowl.
Add celery seed,sugar,vinegar and mayonnaise to bowl.
Salt and pepper to taste.
Stir until well blended.
Refrigerate until chilled and ready to serve.

name: Easy Homemade Classic Coleslaw
directions: Mix all ingedients, except for cabbage.
Whisk until smooth.
Stir in cabbage, chill for at least 1 hour.

name: Spicy Slimmer's Coleslaw
directions: Coarsely chop up the cabbage and carrots and place in a salad bowl.

In another bowl mix salad dressing (do not substitute mayonnaise, it doesn't taste the same), add lemon juice, and hot horseradish.  Try 2 tablespoons of horseradish, taste, if you want it hotter add a little more at a time.  If it's too hot, add a tiny bit more salad dressing.  Mix well.  Taste will cool a little bit once it's added to the veggies.

Next mix into salad bowl containing cabbage and carrots.  We are so used to seeing lots of dressing on our food at first you will think you do not have enough - but mix it in thoroughly and you will have just a light coating of dressing over the veggies.  It tastes great and has far fewer calories than store bought.

name: Oriental Cabbage
directions: Slice onion.  Place in bowl with water.  Cover and microwave on High for 3 minutes.
Put onion in large skillet with remaining ingredients.
Cook, covered, over medium-low heat, stirring occasionally, until cabbage is tender, about 20 minutes.

name: Chinese Chicken Flavored Ramen Noodles Cole Slaw
directions: Although this is delicious without the optional ingredients, I usually add them anyway.
I usually toast my almonds by using the light setting on the toaster oven.
SLAW:
In large bowl, add  slaw mix, green onions, almonds, Craisins, and celery seed. Prepare Dressing and pour over slaw, mixing well.  Refrigerate.
DRESSING.
Before opening Ramen noodle bag, lightly pound the bag to break apart most of the noodles.
In small bowl, add Ramen noodles (including the flavor packet), vinegar, Splenda/sugar, salt, pepper, celery seeds, and bouillon  Stir to mix well. Add canola and sesame oil and stir well.
Note:  You can use a regular head of cabbage in place of Savoy, if you like.

name: Sweet and Sour Cabbage
directions: Preheat the oven to 400F, 200C, gas mark 6.
Shred the cabbage, peel and chop the onions, peel and dice the apples; squeeze the lemons, retaining only the juice.
Crumble the tofu in a large mixing bowl then mix in all the other ingredients.
Place mixture in an oiled casserole dish and first bake for 10 minutes, then lower the oven temperature to 350F, 180C, gas mark 4 and bake for a further 20 minutes.

name: Creamy Bacon Coleslaw (No Pork)
directions: Mix the mayo, honey, vinegar, seasonings together.
Add pecans and bacon.
Add cabbage until it looks like the consistency you like.
Put in frig for at least an hour (bacon tastes like the real thing after a while). Very good.

name: Spectacular Overnight Cole Slaw
directions: In a 4 quart bowl, combine the cabbage, onion, peppers and olives.
In a saucepan, combine remaining ingredients;bring to a boil.
Cook and stir for 1 minute.
Pour over vegetables and stir gently.
Cover and refrigerate overnight.
Mix well before serving.

name: Cabbage and Tomatoes
directions: Core and wash the cabbage.  Cut into 8 sections. (like orange slices).
Place olive oil in a large skillet over med-high heat.
Add garlic to warm oil and sautee 1 minute, add cabbage and stir to coat leaves in oil.
Cook 10-12 minutes or until cabbage is soft.
Add tomatoes and their juice to the cabbage and stir to combine.
Place a lid on the skillet and turn heat down to low.
Allow cabbage and tomatoes to steam for 5-6 minutes.  Add salt and pepper to taste.
Serve immediately.
*Rotel can be used instead of regular tomatoes.

name: Hot Slaw
directions: In a large skillet, cook bacon until crisp. Remove to paper towels to drain. Crumble bacon and set aside.

In reserved bacon fat, cook onions and bell peppers until limp, about 5 minutes. Add shredded cabbage and cook until limp, but not soft, about 3 to 5 minutes. Add chili powder and salt, to taste. Add tomatoes and simmer just until warmed through. Slaw should be slightly crisp. Adjust seasonings. Serve quickly with crumbled bacon.

name: Cheesy Cabbage
directions: Cook cabbage in boiling salted water until tender.
Drain and return to pot.
Add cream and cheese over low heat, stirring constantly until cheese melts and coats cabbage.

name: Tropical Coleslaw
directions: In a large bowl, add sliced cabbage, carrots, and onion.
Add grapefruit sections and toss.
In a small bowl, mix mayonnaise, vinegar, mustard, sugar, salt, and pepper together.
Taste and adjust seasonings, adding more sugar or mustard if needed.
Add dressing to the salad and toss.

name: Cabbage in Milk
directions: Heat milk in saucepan over medium heat.
Add cabbage and zest to milk. Cook about 5 minutes, depending on how firm you like it.
Stir occasionally.
Remove from heat, stir in butter, salt and pepper.
* The type of pepper you use is for your choice. I have tried, fresh black pepper and chili pepper. Didn't care for the chili one.

name: Sweet Simple Coleslaw
directions: For the dressing:
Combine vinegar, oil, sugar, salt, dry mustard and celery seed in a saucepan.
Bring mixture to a boil, stirring to dissolve sugar.
Remove from heat and let cool.
For the salad:
Layer cabbage, onion and bell pepper in a bowl.
Pour sugar over vegetables.
Pour cooled dressing over vegetables.
Chill 3 to 4 hours.
Stir well before serving.
Prep time does not include chill time.

name: Asian Cole Slaw
directions: Very simply, you just mix the cabbage, carrots, ginger, chili, lime zest and juice, the sesame seeds and oils with salt.
add a little sugar to taste.
It will keep in the fridge for up to a couple of days.

name: Craving Coleslaw
directions: Finely chop veggies.  (We used a food processor).
Mix together sugar, pepper, salt, mayo, milk, vinegar and lemon juice.
Pour on cabbage mixture.
Chill and serve.

name: Asian Ramen Cole Slaw Salad
directions: Mix first 5 ingredients and half of the Ramen flavoring packet. Refrigerate.
Crush Ramen noodles and mix with sunflower seeds and almonds.
Mix chopped green onions with slaw mixture.
Stir all ingredients together just before serving.

similar.name: Chinese Chicken Flavored Ramen Noodles Cole Slaw

name: Broccoli Slaw
directions: Mix dressing.
Pour over broccoli and onions.
Refridgerate for an hour or a few hours.
Shortly before serving add in crushed noodles and nuts > toss well.

name: Fried Cabbage With a Kick
directions: Spray a large skillet with non stick spray, heat and add cabbage.
Sprinkle with cayenne pepper (more if you desire).
Garlic salt if you like.
When cabbage begins to warm, add water and cover quickly.
Stir occasionally.
When cabbage is limp and water is almost gone, uncover and cook until slightly browned.
Wonderful with pork chops or beans.

name: Coleslaw With Pecans & Spicy Chile Dressing
directions: Toss cabbage, carrots, apples, red onion, and pecans in large bowl.
In a small bowl, stir together mayonnaise, lemon juice, mustard, sugar, salt, cumin, cayenne and pepper.
Add to the cabbage mixture and toss well to coat.
Cover and refrigerate for at least one hour before serving.

name: KFC Coleslaw by Real Employee
directions: Mix all ingredients except cabbage mix/onion.
Pour over cabbage mix.
Mix well and refrigerate.
Stir a few times before serving.
Slaw best if made several hours before serving. It will get watery after several hours but that is normal. It will keep for 3 day in the fridge.

name: Oriental Ramen Broccoli Slaw
directions: Toss vegetables, peanuts and sunflower seeds.
Combine dressing ingredients in a jar and shake until combined.
Cook and drain the ramen noodles, then run under cold water to cool.
Add noodles to salad mix, then add dressing.  Toss all and allow to rest in the refrigerator for one hour or more.  Flavor gets even better with time!

similar.name: Chinese Chicken Flavored Ramen Noodles Cole Slaw

name: Oriental Cole Slaw
directions: I have had this with the noodles cooked and with them raw.  Raw noodles gives the slaw more crunch, but I prefer them cooked.  You can still have crunch with the seeds and nuts.
Either way, mix together the slaw ingredients in a large container.  Whisk together the dressing ingredients and pour over the slaw mixture.  Toss together.  Cover and refrigerate for an hour.
If you're impatient you can eat it right away.  Keeps for a few days, but it's never around long enough in my house to really find out it's shelf life.

name: Two-Tone Coleslaw (Light & Tasty)
directions: For dressing, in an extra-large bowl stir together light mayonnaise, vinegar, dill, salt, and pepper.
Stir in cabbage, apples, and onion.
Cover, chill at least 2 hours or up to 48 hours.

Note: Use fresh dill if you can. This is a great make ahead side dish, I chill it at least 6 hours before serving and then add more black pepper to taste.  kitchen Tip: When making this salad more than 2 hours ahead of time, toss apple slices in lemon juice to keep them crisp and fresh-looking.

name: Oriental Salad
directions: Mix broccoli slaw, chicken, onions and peppers in a large bowl.  Top with ramen noodles and almonds.
In a seperate bowl, mix sugar, olive oil, soy sauce and vinegar.  Mix really well, as the sugar will thicken.  I usually put it in a bowl with a tight fitting lid and shake it.
Right before serving, pour the dressing over the salad.  Mix well.
Keep refrigerated until serving.

name: KFC Creamy Coleslaw
directions: Mix vegetables in bowl.
Mix remaining ingredients in another bowl.
Pour over top of vegetables.
Mix well.
Cover and refrigerate at least 2 hours before serving.

name: Oriental Salad Makeover
directions: Saute slivered almonds in 1 Tbl butter, set aside.
chop cabbage and onions - mix together. break up Top Ramen into small pieces and add to mixture.
Just before serving, pour 8 oz (about half a 16 oz bottle) of dressing over the top.
Mix, salt and pepper to taste, sprinkle toasted sesame seeds on top. Enjoy ;).

name: Wild West Coleslaw
directions: In a large bowl combine cabbage, apples, grapes, and onion.  Add raisins, walnuts and pickles (if desired).
In a small bowl use a wire whisk to combine mayonnaise, sugar, vinegar, mustard, and salt and pepper, to taste.  Add to cabbage mixture.  Lightly toss until coated.
Cover and refrigerate 2-6 hours before serving. Cover and refrigerate leftovers.

name: Mom's Ohio Coleslaw
directions: Mix together cabbage, onion and bell pepper.
Add salt, pepper and sugar.
Mix or squeeze with hand until mixture is moist.
Add vinegar and salad dressing, mixing thoroughly.
Top with paprika garnish and refrigerate for an hour to allow the flavors to meld.

name: Easy Coleslaw
directions: Place shredded cabbage, carrots and fruit in a bowl and set aside.
Mix mayonnaise, vinegar, sugar and celery seed together well.
Pour over Coleslaw.  Season to taste with salt and pepper.  Chill until time to serve.

name: Chinese Cabbage Salad
directions: Put all salad ingredients in a bowl.
mix dressing together until salt and sugar are dissolved.
pour over salad and toss.
Enjoy it!

name: Best Coleslaw Recipe
directions: Combine slaw mix, shallots, sugar, celery seed.
Add red wine vinegar, stir.
Add mayo, starting with 1/2 cup until desired consistency (creamy).

name: Southwestern Cole Slaw
directions: Place coleslaw mix, onion and Cilantro in a serving bowl, stir to combine. In a separate bowl combine salad dressing, cumin, creole seasoning and pepper.  Just before serving combine salad, dressing, and avacados.

name: Texas Sweet & Sour Cole Slaw
directions: Whisk sugar, vinegar, and oil to blend in large bowl. Add remaining ingredients and toss to combine. Season to taste with salt and pepper. Cover tightly and refrigerate at least 4 hours, tossing occasionally. (Can be prepared 2 days ahead. Keep refrigerated.).

name: Easy Cole Slaw
directions: Combine all ingredients except cole slaw mix in a large bowl and combine well.
Add the cole slaw mix and toss to coat.
Cover and refrigerate several hours or overnight to allow flavors to blend.

name: California Coleslaw
directions: Combine all the salad ingredients in a large bowl.
Combine the dressing ingredients and blend well.
Pour dressing over salad, add salt and pper to taste, toss and serve.

similar.name: Coleslaw With Pecans & Spicy Chile Dressing

name: Crunchy Asian Broccoli Coleslaw
directions: Mix dressing with 1 of the seasoning packages from the soup mix (put 2nd seasoning bag away for another time).
Break apart the noodles; place in bowl and add remaining ingredients; mix.
Add dressing mixture; toss to coat.

name: Oriental Cabbage Slaw
directions: For the  slaw, mix the ingredients together and store in an airtight container in the refrigerator.
For the noodles, crumble the Ramen Noodles into melted margarine in a skillet and brown with the almonds and sesame seeds for approximately 3 minutes. Store in a container in the refrigerator.
For the dressing, mix the ingredients together and store in the refrigerator.

Just before serving, mix all of the ingredients together. If the noodles have been stored in the refrigerator for a while, you may need to let them sit out a few minutes or zap for about 15 seconds in the microwave for the margarine to soften up.

name: Mom's Cole Slaw
directions: Cut cabbage in quarters. Remove core. Cut into thin shreds. Sprinkle with salt and toss. Set aside.
Combine lemon juice, mayonnaise, vinegar, ketchup, and sugar. Drain any accumulated liquid from cabbage. Add carrots and dressing. Toss to combine. Tastes better the next day.

name: Cole Slaw - No Mayo
directions: shred the cabbage into a large bowl.
in separate bowl, prepare the dressing by mixing the vinegars, dijon mustard, sugar, celery seed, caraway seed, and pepper. mix well.
pour over cabbage and mix well. serve chilled.

name: Baked Cabbage
directions: Cook cabbage in boiling water for 8-10 minutes or just until tender.  Drain.
Sprinkle with 1/2 teaspoons salt.
Melt butter and stir in flour, remaining 1/2 teaspoons salt, and pepper.
Add milk and mustard and cook until thickened, stirring constantly.
Combine cabbage, sauce, and half the cheese.
Spoon into buttered 1 quart casserole dish.
Sprinkle with remaining cheese mixed with corn flake crumbs.
Bake at 350 degrees for 20-25 minutes, or until hot and bubbly.

name: Fried Cole Slaw
directions: Melt butter and oil in large skillet over medium-high heat. When foaming subsides, add onion and saute until soft and translucent. Add garlic and saute until fragrant, 30-60 seconds.
Add cabbage, carrots and salt, and cook, tossing frequently, until edges of cabbage begin to brown.
Add pepper, chicken broth and vinegar. Cover tightly and steam 10 minutes until cooked through.

name: Broccoli Slaw Salad
directions: In a small bowl mix together the dressing; mayo, vinegar and sugar. Set Aside.
In large bowl; mix together brocolli, onion, bacon, sunflower seeds and raisins. Toss in the dressing. Can be served immediately or made the night before. Enjoy!

name: Apple Yogurt Coleslaw
directions: Combine first 3 ingredients in a large bowl; toss well and set aside.
Combine next 7 ingredients in a bowl; stir well. Pour over cabbage mixture; toss gently.
Cover and chill 8 hours.

name: Oriental Cabbage
directions: Melt butter in large skillet; add vegetables and cook over medium heat for 10 minutes.
Add evaporated milk and mix.  Remove from heat when evaporated milk is heated.
Season to taste with salt and pepper.

name: Great Broccoli Slaw
directions: Could not be more easy!
Simply break up the noodles and mix with the slaw , add almonds and Craisins
Mix ramen noodle seasoning packets with the other dressing Ingredients and add to slaw.
Chill for best results
Note you can use any slaw that has some broccoli in it, the store I shop at  does not always carry the "Broccoli" slaw , The one they have is called Rainbow Salad! Still comes out great!

name: Cole Slaw Asian Style
directions: combine all ingredients to coat.

name: Skillet Slaw for Two
directions: Fry bacon till crisp; drain and reserve drippings.
Add onion to drippings and cook 2 minutes.
Stir in vinegar, water, sugar, salt, and pepper.
Bring just to boiling.
Add cabbage and apple; toss to coat.
Cover and cook over medium heat 5 minutes or until cabbage wilts.
Stir in sour cream and sprinkle with crumbled bacon.

name: Creamy Coleslaw
directions: Mix all ingredients except cabbage, carrot, and onion in a large plastic or glass bowl.
Add cabbage, carrot, and (optional) onion and toss with a spoon until coated evenly.

Cover and refrigerate for at least one hour, but it's much better if allowed to develop it's sweetness and creaminess overnight. It will compact itself to about 2/3 the volume it was right after tossing it.

name: Cole Slaw
directions: In a large bowl mix together cabbage, onion and carrots.
In separate bowl mix together mayo, oil, vinegar, sugar and salt.
Pour liquid mixture over cabbage mixture and stir.
Refrigerate overnight or for several hours before serving.

name: Red Cabbage
directions: Combine all ingredients in large stainless-steel pot. Bring to a boil, stir, cover, and simmer over low heat, stirring occasionally, for 1 hour and 15 minutes.

name: Fusion Coleslaw
directions: Place the red cabbage, Napa cabbage, carrots, scallion greens, and jalapeno in a large bowl. Stir to combine.

Place the sesame oil, vinegar, sygar, chile sauce, and ginger in a glass jar and seal the lid tightly. Shake the jar vigorously to combine. Taste for seasoning, adding salt and/or more chile garlic sauce as needed.

Pour the dressing over the cabbage mixture and toss to combine. Add the peanuts and raisins and toss again. Refrigerate the coleslaw, covered, to allow the flavors to develop, 2 to 4 hours. Serve the coleslaw chilled, garnished with the sesame seeds.

name: Australian Coleslaw
directions: Add the first 3 ingredients to a bowl.
Mix the remaining ingredients into a sauce and pour over the veggies. Mix thoroughly and refrigerate for at least 2 hours.

name: Broccoli Slaw
directions: Place fresh cut brocoli florets in a large bowl.
Add bacon, cheese, and crasins.
In a seperate bowl, mix mayonaise, vinegar, and sugar thoroughly.
Add may mixture to brocoli mixture.
Cover and let chill for 1-2 hours.
Serve and enjoy!

name: Fred's Cole Slaw
directions: Combine all ingredients.
Enjoy!

name: Easy Coleslaw With a Kick!
directions: Mix everything but the coleslaw together.

Pay special attention to be sure the wasabi is well blended.  A trick I learned that works very well, is to put all the ingredients but the coleslaw mix into a big ziploc bag, then squish, shake, and massage the ingredients into a nice smooth blend.
Then add the coleslaw mix and the ingredients together in a large bowl and mix well.
Cover and put it in the fridge for a couple hours to overnight to allow all the flavors to blend inches  It will be soupy on the bottom, but the salad will be crisp.

name: Warm Pecan Cabbage Slaw
directions: In a large saucepan, combine the cabbage, carrots, green onions, water, salt and pepper.
Cover and cook over medium heat for 5-7 minutes or until cabbage is crisp-tender.
Combine butter and mustard; pour over cabbage mixture.  Stir in pecans.

name: Fresh German Cole Slaw
directions: Shred the cabbage.
Cut bacon into thin strips and fry until crispy.
Chop onion until small.
Mix all ingredients together and eat fresh (cold is best) enjoy!

name: Oriental Cole Slaw
directions: Crush up the ramen noodles and reserve the seasoning packet.
Put ramen noodles, almonds and sesame seeds on a baking sheet and toast in oven at 350 degrees until golden (this works great in a toaster oven too).
Mix the seasoning packet, oil and vinegar in a tightly sealed container and shake vigorously.
In a large bowl, add all ingredients to the cole slaw and toss. Serve immediately.

name: Fancy Cole Slaw
directions: Crunch up uncooked Ramen Noodles and mix in with slaw, onions, almonds, and sunflower seeds.
Combine the dressing ingredients.
Mix everything together, toss and refrigerate 1/2 hour or longer before serving.

name: Easy Cabbage Salsa
directions: mix all ingredients together and refrigerate for 3 hours before serving.

name: Cole Slaw
directions: Shred cabage and mix with all 4 ingredients.

name: Asian Slaw
directions: Crush the noodles and set aside.
Combine cabbage and carrots in a large bowl.
Sprinkle the almonds, sunflower seeds and green onions over all.
In a small bowl, whisk together the sugar, oil, vinegar and the seasoning packets from the noodles - omit the chili powder portion.
Pour the dressing over the cabbage mixture - mix, cover and chill.
Just before serving, mix in the crushed noodles.

name: Oil/Vinegar Coleslaw
directions: Combine all ingredients in medium bowl, cover and refrigerate for at least an hour.

name: Asian Coleslaw
directions: Chop cabbage into small bite size pieces. Grate carrots. Chop onion into fine pieces. Chop celery into fine pieces. Toss into bowl.
Mix vinegar, soya sauce, sesame oil, and sugar. Mix into veggies.
Refrigerate for a few hours before serving.
You can also add dry noodles for a bit of crunch, and a sprinkle of sesame seeds (try them toasted for a bit of flavor)!

name: Simple Cole Slaw
directions: Mix all the ingredients in a bowl and chill (overnight is best).

name: Low Carb Cole Slaw
directions: Place the cabbage in a medium bowl. Mix the remaining ingredients together in a small bowl.
Pour the dressing in with the cabbage, toss to coat. Chill at a least an hour before serving.
Note: The liquid substitute is 1 drop per serving. If using the kind that is 2 drops per serving cut the amount in half. If using Splenda the net carbs will increase.

name: Bint E Naeem's Coleslaw
directions: In a bowl mix all of the ingredients well.
Chill & enjoy :).

name: Country Cole Slaw
directions: Cut vegetables as indicated above.
Add remaining ingredients.
Combine and chill.

name: Janice's Overnight Cabbage Slaw
directions: Not sure of exact size of slaw mix. It is the large bag.
Combine cole slaw mix and onion in a bowl with a lid. Set aside.
Combine remaining ingredients in a sauce pan. Mix and bring to a boil. After all is dissolved, pour hot dressing mixture over slaw mix in bowl. DO NOT STIR!
Cover and refrigerate for 24 hours.
Stir and serve.
Prep and cook time does not include 24 hour refrigeration.
Will last for weeks.

name: Cole Slaw
directions: Stir shredded cabbage, onion, celery, celery seed, salt, mayonnaise, and tomato together.
Cover and refrigerate.

name: Lisa's Broccoli Slaw
directions: Fry and crumble bacon.  Drain.  Mix mayo, sugar & vinegar. Pour over slaw; mix in bacon, sunflower seeds and raisins.  Chill and enjoy!

name: Aunt Sarah's Cole Slaw
directions: In a small mixing bowl, combine mayonnaise, sweet milk, vinegar and sugar. Blend thoroughly. Add remaining ingredients and toss gently until evenly coated.

name: Southwest Broccoli Slaw
directions: Combine first 5 ingredients to make the dressing.
Add chopped green onion, diced jalapeno and broccoli slaw.
Chill for 1 hour, minimum.

name: Creamy Coleslaw
directions: Combine the first 6 ingredients in a bowl, and stir until combined smoothly.
Add the coleslaw mix, or cabbage and toss until well coated with dressing.
Chill and serve.

name: Low-Carb Creamy Coleslaw
directions: Mix first six ingredients in a medium sized bowl until blended.
Stir in the bag of coleslaw blend.
Refrigerate for 30 min to 1 hour before serving.

name: Cole Slaw
directions: Mix all ingredients.
Marinate for two hours. (NOTE Over Night Prefered).

name: Cole Slaw - Picnic Safe
directions: Put cabbage, onion and carrot in a bowl and toss.
Whisk together the dressing ingredients.
Pour dressing over the veggies and mix well.

name: No Sugar Primanti Style Coleslaw
directions: Combined the Splenda and apple cider vinegar in a bowl and whisk until combined. Place cabbage in the ziplock bag and then pour the dressing in to the bag and add pepper to taste. Massage the bag to spread the dressing and place in the fridge for at least an hour. The longer it sits the better it gets.
